name: Yan Baihu
page_title: Yan Baihu “White Tiger Yan” 嚴白虎 “白虎嚴” [Yan Baihu] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
historical_notes:
Known as White Tiger Yan, he was a local leader in the hills of Wu commandery, south of Qu’a. He formed a loose confederation that allied with Taschi Ci’s attempts to form a rival administration to Sun Ce’s. When Xu Gong was defeated by Sun Ce, he fled to Yan Baihu but only after Wang Lang surrendered did Sun Ce turn his attention to Yan Baihu.

Sun Ce defeated the confederation but Yan Baihu escaped to regroup his forces. Chen Yu worked secretly with Yan Baihu to destroy Sun Ce but the plan was defeated when Lü Fan attacked Chen Yu while Sun Ce led an expedition against Yan Baihu who escaped. Yan Baihu revolted again on Chen Deng’s encouragement, Sun Ce assassinated before he could launch a campaign against Yan Baihu.
novel_notes:
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
	
		
	Yan Baihu was not killed by Dong Xi, he was still alive when Sun Ce was killed.

sections.short-biography: Fought with Sun Ce, but was defeated. Killed by Dong Xi.
sections.details:
Nickname 
	“White Tiger Yan” (Baihuyan 白虎嚴)


	 Traditional Chinese 
	嚴白虎 “白虎嚴”


	 Simplified Chinese 
	严白虎


	 Pronunciation 
	Yan2 Bai2hu3 


	 Pinyin (Tones) 
	Yán Báihǔ “Báihǔyán”


	 Wade-Giles 
	Yen Paihu [White Tiger Yen] “Pai-hu-yen”

	 Birthplace 
	Wu

	 Other Names 
	White Tiger of Yan, White Tiger Yan, White Tiger King, White Tiger

	 Name Notes 
	‘白虎’ translates to ‘White Tiger’, thus his alleged Chinese name of Yan Baihu actually reads ‘White Tiger of Yan’, as he appears in several English books. Yan Baihu was recorded in history by his nickname. His real name, if he actually had one, is unknown.

full_text:
Yan Baihu “White Tiger Yan” 嚴白虎 “白虎嚴”
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Yan Baihu (嚴白虎)aka“White Tiger Yan” (白虎嚴)was born in Wu. Fought with Sun Ce, but was defeated. Killed by Dong Xi.

BiographyHistoric (Confirmed)
Known as White Tiger Yan, he was a local leader in the hills of Wu commandery, south of Qu’a. He formed a loose confederation that allied with Taschi Ci’s attempts to form a rival administration to Sun Ce’s. When Xu Gong was defeated by Sun Ce, he fled to Yan Baihu but only after Wang Lang surrendered did Sun Ce turn his attention to Yan Baihu.
Sun Ce defeated the confederation but Yan Baihu escaped to regroup his forces. Chen Yu worked secretly with Yan Baihu to destroy Sun Ce but the plan was defeated when Lü Fan attacked Chen Yu while Sun Ce led an expedition against Yan Baihu who escaped. Yan Baihu revolted again on Chen Deng’s encouragement, Sun Ce assassinated before he could launch a campaign against Yan Baihu.
